common.tagline: Every story is a map of the stories it could have been.

login.intro: Sign the register, archivist. A sealed link will be sent to your name.
login.sentTitle: An 8-digit code is on its way to {email}. Enter it below to enter the archive.
login.codeError: That code didn't open the door. Check it and try again.
login.caption: No password. The desert keeps your secrets.

archive.title: The Archive
archive.emptyBody: Every dig begins with a single sealed scroll. Unearth your first tale and watch its timelines branch.

atlas.label: Story atlas — the tree of timelines
atlas.title: Story Atlas

reader.noBranches: No forks yet. This passage is where the trail ends.
reader.steerPlaceholder: …but she refuses the offer and walks into the storm.
reader.forkFailed: The fork would not take. Try again.
reader.quotaExceeded: You've used your free passages for now. They'll refresh as your 30-day window rolls forward.
reader.passagesLeft: {count, plural, one {# free passage left} other {# free passages left}}

create.title: Begin a new scroll
create.intro: Give your story its first spark. Alexandria unrolls the opening passage — every page after is a fork.
create.sparkPlaceholder: A lighthouse keeper finds a door in the sea-fog that wasn't there yesterday…
create.sparkError: Your story needs a spark. Write a premise to begin.
create.failed: The scroll would not open. Try again.

branching.title: A story is a tree
branching.body: This isn't one path — it's all of them. Any passage can fork into its own timeline, and they coexist. These are the branches growing from this passage.

steer.title: Steer the next passage
steer.body: Before you fork, whisper a nudge here — "but she refuses the offer" — and the new branch grows the way you point.

atlas.title: Open the Atlas
atlas.body: The Atlas maps every timeline as a tree. Tap any node to leap there instantly — revisiting is always free.

quota.title: Your branch allowance
quota.body: New branches draw on a free allowance — 20 every 30 days. Revisiting passages you've already written never counts.

create.title: Now, your turn
create.body: This was just a demo tale. Unearth your own premise and watch its timelines branch.

demo.readonly: A guided demo — forking is off here. Switch its language below, or begin your own story.

hero.subhead: Every story is a map of the stories it could have been.

tree.title: A story is a tree, not a line.
tree.body: Most fiction marches in one direction. Alexandria branches. Reach a turning point and you don't pick a single path — you take every path. Each fork becomes its own living timeline, and they all coexist.
tree.caption: one premise · many timelines

how.title: Four moves, infinite stories.
how.sparkBody: Give it a single sentence — a genre, a tone, a what-if. Alexandria writes the opening passage.
how.readBody: Each beat is about 250 words. Short enough that the tree, not the page count, is the experience.
how.forkBody: Nudge the next passage — "but she refuses the offer" — and a new branch grows from where you stood.
how.revisitBody: Every passage is frozen once written. Jump back to any timeline with zero waiting and zero cost.

why.title: A library you build, not output you discard.
why.frozenBody: Revisiting a passage is instant and free — no regeneration, no surprise bills. The story you wrote stays exactly as you left it.
why.allowanceBody: Every account comes with a built-in allowance of new passages — enough to grow real timelines before you ever think about cost.
why.yoursBody: No feeds, no followers, no algorithm. Just you and the tree of everything your story could become.

faq.title: The short version.
faq.publicA: Alexandria is in public beta. Sign in with a magic link and start writing — no waitlist.
faq.keyQ: Do I need to pay to start?
faq.keyA: No. Every account comes with a free allowance of new passages to get you going — no setup, no card required.
faq.languagesQ: What languages can I write in?
faq.languagesA: English or Brazilian Portuguese, chosen per story — independent of the language you read the site in.
faq.oneshotQ: Is this an AI book generator?
faq.oneshotA: No. Alexandria doesn't spit out a finished novel. It grows a branching tree you explore and steer, one passage at a time.
